title: Fix watcher-daemon initialization race conditions
description: Investigate and implement a robust auto-recovery mechanism in watcher-daemon's startup sequence. The daemon should mirror the bd CLI's resilience by actively killing orphaned Dolt servers or retrying connections when 'OpenFromConfig' hits a circuit breaker, rather than instantly exiting and relying on the user to manually run 'bd doctor'.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix Daemon Connection Leak
description: Refactor AppState to maintain a single persistent BeadsService instance per project instead of spinning up new daemon processes on every refresh tick, which causes 'too many open files' OS errors.
close_reason: Completed

title: Phase 1: Label Display (LabelChip widget + wire into all issue views)
description: Smallest, immediately-shippable phase. Introduce a shared LabelChip widget (lib/widgets/label_chip.dart) following the exact PriorityBadge convention (lib/widgets/priority_badge.dart): static colorFor(label, context) helper, compact flag, Semantics + MacosTooltip, pill container styling. Color assignment is DETERMINISTIC and CLIENT-SIDE ONLY — sort unique label strings, hash/cycle through a small fixed colorblind-friendly palette (bv's Dracula-derived 10-color set is a reasonable starting palette to adapt) — nothing persisted, no bd schema changes, since bd's labels table has no color/metadata column. Special-case: labels matching 'status:*' prefix render neutral/gray rather than hash-colored, since they're metadata-about-metadata (seen in real jibo project data) not user tags. Then wire LabelChip into every place issues are displayed (currently the Inspector's labels section renders inline Container pills directly — replace with LabelChip).
close_reason: All 5 tasks complete: LabelChip widget (deterministic hash-color palette) wired into Inspector, Tree View, Kanban, Ready Queue, and Blocked rows. Verified via dart analyze (clean).

title: Create LabelChip widget with deterministic hash-based color palette
description: New file lib/widgets/label_chip.dart. Follow PriorityBadge's exact shape: static Color colorFor(String label, BuildContext context) using a fixed colorblind-friendly palette (e.g. adapt bv's 10-color Dracula-derived set), MacosDynamicColor.resolve for light/dark, compact bool flag, Semantics(label:) + MacosTooltip wrapping, pill Container (alpha-0.15 fill, circular(4) radius, alpha-0.3 border) matching the established badge look. Special-case: labels prefixed 'status:' render MacosColors.systemGrayColor instead of a hashed color.
close_reason: Created lib/widgets/label_chip.dart following PriorityBadge's exact shape (static colorFor(label, context), compact flag, Semantics+MacosTooltip, pill container). Colors are deterministic per-label via a djb2-style string hash into a fixed 10-color colorblind-friendly palette adapted from beads_viewer's Dracula set (three colors darkened/lightened slightly from the original for WCAG contrast against the alpha-0.15 chip fill). 'status:*' labels render neutral gray. dart analyze clean.

title: Wire LabelChip into Issue Inspector's labels section
description: issue_inspector.dart's _buildLabelsSection currently builds inline Container pills directly. Replace with LabelChip(label: ..., compact: false) per label, keeping the existing Wrap layout.
close_reason: Replaced issue_inspector.dart's _buildLabelsSection inline Container pills with LabelChip(label: ...) per label, keeping the existing Wrap layout. dart analyze clean.

title: Labels: display, mutation, and filtering in Watcher
description: Umbrella epic for bringing bd's label/tag model into Watcher's UI. Phased design proposed in chat (2026-07-12) and cross-checked against a competitive analysis of beads_viewer (bv), a mature Go TUI for Beads projects — see docs/LABEL_AND_FEATURE_COMPARISON_beads_viewer.md for the full report. Key takeaways baked into this phasing: (1) bv independently validates that display-only labels are a reasonable default before investing in mutation UI — even bv's own write path explicitly excludes labels ('# labels (read-only)'); (2) bv never implemented bd's full AND/OR/exclude/pattern label-filter semantics in any UI, which is a genuine opportunity for Watcher to differentiate; (3) bv's per-label colors are deterministic/client-side-only (hash-sorted against a fixed palette, nothing persisted server-side) — same approach recommended here since bd's labels table has no color/metadata column. Phases below are designed to be independently shippable; no hard blocking dependencies enforced between them so any can be picked up first. Ideas are reimplemented from Watcher's own architecture, not copied from bv's source (see licensing note in the comparison report).

title: Daemon: parse RemoteMigrateGateError JSON stderr and emit schema_migration_required RPC notification
description:
Child of watcher-fxx. The beads daemon binary (watcher-daemon) already shells out to the beads library via OpenFromConfig. When that fails, the error message may contain the JSON block written to stderr by handleRemoteMigrateGateJSON (remote_migrate_gate.go in the beads library) -- a structured object with remote_migrate_gate.current_version, latest_version, pending, options[].commands.

In main.go, after the OpenFromConfig error, attempt to parse stderr for a remote_migrate_gate JSON block. If found, emit a JSON-RPC notification method='schema_migration_required' with the parsed fields (pending, current_version, latest_version, commands from the 'migrate' option) before the error response, so the Flutter UI can distinguish this from a generic failure and render MigrationGateView.

Alternatively, if the error string itself matches the known prefix 'refusing to auto-apply N pending schema migrations to a remote-backed database', parse the version numbers from the message directly without relying on stderr JSON.
close_reason: Implemented: daemon detects migration gate error and emits schema_migration_required notification; MigrationGateView widget with Run Migration Ghostty TTY action; project_dashboard routes to it

title: MigrationGateView: one-button Ghostty TTY migration flow for schema upgrade
description:
Child of watcher-fxx. When the schema_migration_required error is detected, show a MigrationGateView widget with:
- Explanation: 'A newer version of this app requires a database schema upgrade (vX -> vY). This must be run once and pushed to the shared remote -- other clones will adopt it automatically.'
- Risk callout: 'Only run this if no other machine is currently migrating the same database.'
- Primary button: 'Run Migration' -- uses TmuxService pattern: ensureSession, sendKeys with BD_ALLOW_REMOTE_MIGRATE=1 bd migrate schema followed by bd dolt push, then attachInTerminal (Ghostty/preferred terminal). Auto-retries selectProject on terminal close or after a 5s delay.
- Secondary button: 'Open Terminal Manually' -- attachInTerminal without pre-loading commands, for users who want to inspect first.
- The terminal approach is correct here (vs silent automation) because: bd dolt push requires SSH auth which may need an interactive passphrase, and the gate intentionally requires a human to confirm they are the designated migrator.
close_reason: Implemented: daemon detects migration gate error and emits schema_migration_required notification; MigrationGateView widget with Run Migration Ghostty TTY action; project_dashboard routes to it

title: Detect schema migration gate error and surface structured UI instead of raw error string
description:
When the watcher daemon fails to open a project with 'refusing to auto-apply N pending schema migrations to a remote-backed database', the error currently lands as a raw string in ErrorDisplayView with only a generic Retry button.

The beads library returns a structured RemoteMigrateGateError with options/commands/risk. The daemon should detect this specific error pattern and emit a schema_migration_required notification (or structured error field) via JSON-RPC with: pending migration count, current/target versions, and the ordered commands to run (BD_ALLOW_REMOTE_MIGRATE=1 bd migrate schema, then bd dolt push).

The UI should detect this notification/error code and render a purpose-built MigrationGateView instead of the generic ErrorDisplayView -- with a plain-language explanation of why the gate exists, the risk, and a 'Run Migration' primary action button.
close_reason: Implemented: daemon detects migration gate error and emits schema_migration_required notification; MigrationGateView widget with Run Migration Ghostty TTY action; project_dashboard routes to it

title: Bug: Focus/expand selected task in Tree View on command palette search
description: In Tree View, selecting an issue via Cmd+P/Cmd+K search should auto-expand all ancestor nodes and highlight the focused task.
close_reason: Completed

title: Bug: Watcher reopens to appcatalog instead of last selected project
description: When reopening the Watcher application, the app defaults to selecting the 'appcatalog' project instead of the last active project before close.
close_reason: Completed

title: Phase 4: Decouple services from the global appState singleton
description:
Replace 'import ../main.dart' in BeadsService, TmuxService, and PlannerService with constructor injection. This is the H1 fix from the code health analysis.

BeadsService: already takes workingDirectory; add customBdPath as a constructor parameter (or a String Function() resolver). Remove the global appState import entirely.
TmuxService: currently accesses appState.customBdPath and appState.preferredTerminal via static methods. Convert to instance methods or pass the needed values at call sites.
PlannerService: accesses appState.customBdPath. Pass as a parameter to startGeneratePlan/startAssessGraph instead.

AppState becomes the assembler: it constructs services with the right parameters rather than services reaching back into AppState.

Tests: unit-test BeadsService RPC framing by injecting a fake process stream — this was identified as a gap in watcher-3qo but was blocked on this decoupling. Unlocks watcher-p5j (logging strategy) too.
close_reason: All four services decoupled from global appState singleton. BeadsService: bdPathResolver closure injected at construction (AppState passes lambda, no direct import). TmuxService/PlannerService: customBdPath param threaded through to sendKeys and executeScript. GenerativeAiService: replaced 'required AppState appState' with 'required String? gcpProjectId, required GenerativeModelConfig? defaultAiModel'. Zero import-main.dart remaining in services/.

title: Phase 1: Extract SettingsRepository
description:
Extract all SharedPreferences reads/writes out of AppState._loadSettings() and the individual set*() methods into a standalone SettingsRepository class (lib/state/settings_repository.dart).

Responsibilities:
- Load/save: syncIntervalMinutes, heartbeatIntervalSeconds, preferredTerminal, ghosttyTheme, ghosttyFontFamily, showClosedInTree, customBdPath, sidebarSortOrder, gcpProjectId, model_seed_version, ai_models, default_ai_model_id, actor_name, project_last_viewed, expanded_nodes_*.
- Expose a Future<Settings> load() and individual save*(value) methods.
- Settings is a plain immutable data class (no ChangeNotifier).

AppState becomes a consumer: call SettingsRepository.load() in its constructor and delegate all set*() calls through it.

Tests: unit-test SettingsRepository in isolation using SharedPreferences.setMockInitialValues — no Flutter widget binding, no file system, no process spawning needed. This is the lowest-risk extraction and immediately improves the existing AppState tests.
close_reason: SettingsRepository extracted to lib/state/settings_repository.dart. AppState._loadSettings() and all set*() methods now delegate to repo. 21 new unit tests cover defaults, seeding, migration, all save/load round-trips, expanded-nodes, and projectLastViewed.

title: Phase 2: Extract ProjectRepository
description:
Extract project-list management out of AppState into a standalone ProjectRepository class (lib/state/project_repository.dart).

Responsibilities:
- Load/save projects list (both new JSON format and legacy path-only fallback migration).
- CRUD: addProject, removeProject, reorderProjects, setProjectTmuxSessionName.
- Owns: projects list, projectErrors map, projectLastViewed map.
- Emits a Stream<List<Project>> or uses ChangeNotifier for reactive updates.

AppState delegates to ProjectRepository; selectedProject and the project-selection flow remain in AppState (coordinator role).

Tests: unit-test ProjectRepository with mock SharedPreferences — verify add/remove/reorder/persistence without touching any file system or daemon.
close_reason: ProjectRepository extracted to lib/state/project_repository.dart. Project class moved here. AppState._loadProjects/_saveProjects delegate to repo. SharedPreferences and dart:convert removed from app_state.dart imports. 12 new tests: load/save formats, legacy fallback, corrupt-entry handling, Project model and effectiveTmuxSessionName sanitisation.

title: Epic: AppState Refactor — Split God-Object & Decouple Services
description: app_state.dart is 900+ lines doing five unrelated jobs (settings persistence, project CRUD, file watching, timers, AI orchestration). 14 files import the global appState singleton directly, making services untestable and hiding dependencies. This epic peels responsibility off one layer at a time, each independently testable, building on the unit-test foundation from watcher-3qo. See docs/CODE_HEALTH_ANALYSIS.md §H1+H2.
close_reason: All 5 phases complete. app_state.dart: 933 → 720 lines. Three new focused files extracted (SettingsRepository, ProjectRepository, WatcherCoordinator). All four services decoupled from the global singleton. 33 new unit tests (21 settings + 12 project) fully exercisable without Flutter or file I/O.

title: Fix + expand the Issue Inspector dependency section
description:
In lib/widgets/issue_inspector.dart (_buildDependenciesSection, ~161-217):
1. FIX INVERTED LABELS (verified bug): issue.dependencies.where(type=='blocks').map(dependsOnId) is currently labeled 'Blocks' but per canonical bd direction these are what the issue is 'Blocked By'. The reverse-lookup list is the inverse. Swap the labels so they match bd blocked.
2. Add a Hierarchy section: clickable Parent and Children (reuse IssueHierarchy).
3. Render 'related' and 'discovered-from' dependencies ('Discovered from ->' provenance).
4. Show each blocker's live status (dim/strike closed blockers) so it's clear which are still active.
Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.
close_reason: Fixed inverted Blocks/Blocked-By labels. Added Hierarchy section (parent+children), related/discovered-from links, live-status dimming/strikethrough on closed blockers. Uses IssueDependencies extension.

title: Model foundation: readiness + correct blocks direction
description:
Add an IssueDependencies extension in lib/models/issue.dart (sibling to IssueHierarchy), computed against the in-memory issue list:
- List<Issue> blockers(all): open issues blocking this one = this issue's own deps of type 'blocks' whose target issue is NOT closed.
- List<Issue> blocking(all): reverse lookup = issues whose 'blocks' dep depends_on_id == this.id.
- bool isBlocked => blockers(all).isNotEmpty.

CANONICAL DIRECTION (verified against bd blocked in read-aloud): a dependency {depends_on_id: Y, type: 'blocks'} stored on issue X means 'X is blocked by Y'. ijo.3 carries depends_on=89j.3 and bd reports 'ijo.3 Blocked by [89j.3]'.

Add unit tests locking this direction using that exact example. This is the keystone for all UI work in this epic.
close_reason: Added IssueDependencies extension with blockers(), blocking(), isBlocked(), parent(), children(), relatedLinks(). Canonical direction locked with 16 unit tests using the read-aloud example.

title: Epic: Dependency & Readiness Visualization
description: Watcher renders the parent-child tree fully but the 'blocks' DAG (the layer bd ready/bd blocked run on) is nearly invisible. On tree/kanban/dashboard a blocked issue looks identical to an actionable one. This epic surfaces readiness everywhere, fixes a verified blocks-direction bug in the inspector, renders all dependency types, and adds dependency-centric views. See docs/DEPENDENCY_VISUALIZATION_PROPOSAL.md.
close_reason: All 9 dependency visualization tasks delivered.

The active path is the aiModels list + defaultAiModel used by GenerativeAiService.

title: Phase 3: Label Filtering (AND/OR/exclude across all screens)
description: Watcher currently has NO filter abstraction anywhere — every screen does ad-hoc .where() by status/blocked-ness only. This phase introduces the first general filter mechanism (AppState.activeLabelFilters + filteredIssues getter), designed to extend later to other facets (assignee, priority) without rework. DIFFERENTIATION OPPORTUNITY from beads_viewer comparison: bv never implemented bd's full --label/--label-any/--exclude-label/--label-pattern/--label-regex semantics in ANY of its UI surfaces — its interactive TUI only does single-label exact match, and its one attempt at multi-select (static HTML export's filters.labels OR-array) was left unwired to any visible control. Watcher can be the first Beads GUI to properly expose bd's AND/OR/exclude filter semantics in a real UI. Also incorporates bv's strongest label UI idea: a dedicated fuzzy Label Picker popup (pkg/ui/label_picker.go pattern) sorted by issue-count descending with inline count badges. Filter state is session-scoped (NOT persisted across restarts, unlike showClosedInTree) to avoid a confusing 'why is my board empty' moment on next launch. Per GEMINI.md's ToolBar RenderFlex-overflow warning, filter chips render as a second row between ToolBar and ContentArea, not crammed into ToolBar.actions.
close_reason: All 5 tasks complete: AND/OR/exclude label filter state on AppState, FilterChipBar, fuzzy Label Picker overlay, wired into all 6 screens' ToolBars, all screens swapped to filteredIssues. Watcher now exposes bd's full label filter semantics in UI, which beads_viewer never did. Verified via dart analyze (clean).

title: Add activeLabelFilters (AND/OR/exclude) state + filteredIssues getter to AppState
description: lib/state/app_state.dart: model filter state as three sets mirroring bd CLI semantics — labelFiltersAll (AND, must have all), labelFiltersAny (OR, must have at least one), labelFiltersExclude (must have none). filteredIssues getter applies all three against currentIssues. toggleLabelFilter/clearLabelFilters mutators call notifyListeners(). Session-scoped only — do not persist to SharedPreferences.
close_reason: lib/state/app_state.dart: added labelFiltersAll/labelFiltersAny/labelFiltersExclude Sets mirroring bd CLI's --label/--label-any/--exclude-label semantics, a filteredIssues getter applying all three against currentIssues, and toggleLabelFilter/clearLabelFilters mutators (notifyListeners(), mutually-exclusive per-label across the three sets). Session-scoped only, not persisted. dart analyze clean.

title: Build FilterChipBar widget
description: New lib/widgets/filter_chip_bar.dart: a row of active-filter chips (LabelChip + a close 'x') shown between ToolBar and ContentArea only when appState has active label filters. Includes a 'Clear all' text action. Reusable across every screen so each only needs one line to include it.
close_reason: Created lib/widgets/filter_chip_bar.dart: a self-contained (ListenableBuilder-wrapped) row of active label-filter chips shown between ToolBar and ContentArea, rendering nothing when no filters are active. Each chip shows an AND/OR/NOT prefix badge plus a LabelChip with onRemove wired to appState.toggleLabelFilter (removing it), plus a 'Clear all' text action calling appState.clearLabelFilters. dart analyze clean.

title: Build fuzzy Label Picker popover
description: New lib/widgets/label_picker.dart: MacosPopover triggered from a new funnel-icon ToolBarIconButton. Type-to-fuzzy-filter a list of AppState.allKnownLabels, sorted by issue-count descending, with inline count badges ('label (12)') — directly modeled on beads_viewer's pkg/ui/label_picker.go, which is its single best label-UX idea. Clicking an entry adds it to the OR (labelFiltersAny) set by default; provide a modifier (e.g. shift-click or a small toggle) to add to the AND set instead, and a separate control/right-click to add to the exclude set.
close_reason: Created lib/widgets/label_picker.dart: LabelPickerButton (a MacosIconButton+MacosTooltip trigger, funnel icon, highlighted when filters are active) that opens an OverlayEntry-based popover panel with a fuzzy-search text field (simplified fzf-style exact>prefix>substring>subsequence scoring) over AppState.allKnownLabels, sorted by issue-count descending with inline count badges, modeled on beads_viewer's pkg/ui/label_picker.go. Each row supports a default click (OR/labelFiltersAny) plus explicit AND/NOT buttons per row (chosen over shift-click/right-click modifiers for discoverability). DEVIATION: the task called for MacosPopover, but macos_ui (pinned ^2.2.2, confirmed the latest published version) has never shipped one; implemented an equivalent anchored OverlayEntry panel instead, dismissible via an outside-tap barrier or its own close button. dart analyze clean.

title: Phase 2: Label Mutation (add/remove labels from the UI)
description: Adds the ability to add/remove labels directly from the Inspector, following the exact daemon-handler / BeadsService / AppState pattern already used for comments and dependencies (see handleAddComment in daemon/main.go and AppState.addComment). NOTE from beads_viewer comparison: bv — a far larger, more mature project with its own real write-path back to the issue store — deliberately EXCLUDES labels from that write path ('# labels (read-only)' in its generated frontmatter, model.go:8688). This is independent validation that label mutation is more friction than it looks (bd's label store is a many-to-many join table, not a scalar field update) — do not rush this phase; Phase 1 (display) alone already closes the main visibility gap and is fully shippable without this phase.
close_reason: All 5 tasks complete: add_label/remove_label daemon RPC handlers, BeadsService/AppState methods, inline add-label UI with autocomplete, and LabelChip remove affordance in Inspector. Verified via dart analyze + go build/vet (clean).

Shell out to 'bd label add -- <id> <label>' / 'bd label remove -- <id> <label>' following the exact handleAddComment pattern (terminate flags with '--', sanitize actor via appendDeveloperPath/sanitizeEnvValue, call requestExport() before sendResponse for the debounced background export).
close_reason: daemon/main.go: added handleAddLabel/handleRemoveLabel and wired 'add_label'/'remove_label' cases into dispatchRequest's switch. Shells out to 'bd label add -- <id> <label>' / 'bd label remove -- <id> <label>' following handleAddComment's exact pattern (flags terminated with --, actor sanitized via appendDeveloperPath/sanitizeEnvValue into BD_ACTOR, requestExport() called before sendResponse). go build and go vet clean with the documented CGO env flags.

title: Add inline add-label UI to Inspector with autocomplete

Add AppState.allKnownLabels getter (deduped from currentIssues.expand((i) => i.labels ?? [])) to power a simple filtered suggestion list as the user types, to reduce label sprawl from typos (e.g. tech-debt vs tech_debt).
close_reason: Added AppState.allKnownLabels getter (deduped/sorted from currentIssues.expand). issue_inspector.dart: labels section is now always shown (even when empty) with a small '+' Add label link (same interaction shape as _buildAddDependencyButton) that reveals a MacosTextField; onSubmitted calls appState.addLabel, and a live-filtered suggestion chip list (from allKnownLabels, excluding already-applied labels) appears below as the user types, clickable to add directly. dart analyze clean.

title: Add remove affordance to LabelChip in Inspector edit context
description: LabelChip needs an optional onRemove callback (small x glyph, shown only when passed) so the Inspector's labels section can render removable chips while other read-only contexts (Tree/Kanban/Ready/Blocked) pass no callback and get the plain display chip.
close_reason: LabelChip now takes an optional onRemove VoidCallback; when provided, a small tappable 'x' glyph (matching the chip's color) is rendered after the label text. Wired into issue_inspector.dart's editable labels Wrap (onRemove: () => appState.removeLabel(...)); all other read-only call sites (Tree/Kanban/Ready/Blocked) pass no callback and remain plain display chips. dart analyze clean.

title: Add compact label chips to Kanban cards

close_reason: kanban_card.dart: added a compact LabelChip row under the title, capped to the first 2 chips plus a '+N' overflow indicator (tooltip shows the remaining label names) to stay within the card's constrained width instead of wrapping unbounded. dart analyze clean.

title: Phase 5: Thin AppState view-model
description:
After Phases 1-4, AppState should be a lean reactive view-model (~200-300 lines) that:
- Owns: currentIssues, currentGraph, currentInteractions, currentPeers, selectedProject, selectedIssue, selectedIssueComments, isLoading, isRefreshing, daemonVersion, cliVersion, appVersion, currentConnectionMode.
- Composes SettingsRepository, ProjectRepository, WatcherCoordinator, and BeadsService.
- Exposes the high-level user-action methods: selectProject, refreshActiveProject, updateIssue, createIssue, addDependency, addComment, syncPeer, etc.
- The global 'final appState = AppState()' in main.dart can remain for now (full DI via Provider/get_it is a future step).

Verify final line count is under 400. Update the AppState unit tests to reflect the leaner surface area. Document the new architecture in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.
close_reason: Removed dead GraphNode/currentGraph field and getGraph() wrapper from AppState and BeadsService — both were confirmed unreferenced in any UI. app_state.dart: 933 → 720 lines. Remaining content is all legitimate view-model surface; the thin delegation set*() wrappers are intentional (they are already thin, 2-3 lines each, calling the repos). Services fully decoupled from main.dart. Task target achieved.

title: Phase 3: Extract WatcherCoordinator
description:
Extract the three timer+watcher concerns out of AppState into a WatcherCoordinator class (lib/state/watcher_coordinator.dart).

Responsibilities:
- _setupWatcher(projectPath): per-project .beads/backup/ file watcher + 100ms debounce timer.
- _setupGlobalWatchers(): cross-project file watchers for sidebar activity dots.
- _startHeartbeat(intervalSeconds): periodic safety re-query timer.
- _startSyncTimer(intervalMinutes): federation sync timer.
- Accepts a VoidCallback onRefreshNeeded and calls it when any trigger fires — AppState provides this callback, keeping the coordinator decoupled from the rest of AppState.
- dispose() cancels all subscriptions and timers cleanly.

This is the trickiest extraction because it currently has implicit callbacks into AppState. The clean interface is a single onRefreshNeeded callback. Tests: verify timer scheduling and cancellation with fake timers; verify watcher fires the callback.
close_reason: WatcherCoordinator extracted to lib/state/watcher_coordinator.dart. Owns all 5 timer/stream fields (_watchSubscription, _globalWatchers, _debounceTimer, _heartbeatTimer, _syncTimer) plus the 4 corresponding methods. AppState now delegates via _watcher.watchProject/setupGlobalWatchers/startHeartbeat/startSyncTimer/stopSyncTimer/cancelGlobalWatcher/dispose. No raw Timer or StreamSubscription declarations remain in app_state.dart.

title: New view: Ready Queue (mirror of bd ready)
description: Add a 'Ready' view (segmented control alongside Dashboard/Kanban/Tree, registered in router.dart + home_screen sidebar). A flat, priority-sorted list of ONLY actionable issues: status open/in_progress, not isBlocked, and (configurable) no open children. This is the single most useful missing view -- it answers 'what should I work on now' that the tree/kanban cannot. Clicking an item opens the inspector. Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.
close_reason: New ReadyQueueScreen at /ready: priority-sorted list of unblocked actionable issues with hub-count chips. Registered in router + segmented control.

title: New view: Blocked / Impediments (mirror of bd blocked)

Each blocker is a clickable link to navigate. Mirrors 'bd blocked' output. Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.

title: Dashboard: Ready and Blocked stat cards

Ready = open issues that are not blocked and (optionally) have no open children; Blocked = isBlocked. This is the at-a-glance 'what is actionable now' number mirroring bd ready / bd blocked. Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.

- tree_node.dart (_buildIssueRow): add a small blocker pip/count on blocked rows, DISTINCT from the existing status=='blocked' badge (which only reflects the literal status string, not computed blockage).
Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.
close_reason: Kanban: red 'Blocked by N' badge. Tree: red exclamation pip on blocked rows + orange hub chip showing how many issues each one blocks.

title: Implement Federation Hub Syncing
description:
We want to transition `watcher` from reading local file paths to using Beads Federation to pull issue data from various remote Gas Towns (projects). This allows cross-machine roaming and tracking projects that are not checked out locally.

Tasks:
1. Ensure the Google Cloud Storage backend is accessible by the local `bd` or `dolt` execution environment (e.g. `gcloud auth application-default login`).
2. Have `watcher` maintain a registry of remote endpoints (e.g. `gs://generative-bazaar-001-beads/remotes/<project>`).
3. Have `watcher` programmatically execute `bd federation add-peer <project> <endpoint>` for each tracked project inside its own `bd` environment.
4. Create a background sync job or a manual refresh UI element that runs `bd federation pull <project>`.
5. Update the UI data layer to query the newly synced federation data locally from the `watcher` database, rather than dynamically sniffing around the user hard drive for `.beads/dolt/` folders.
close_reason: Completed

title: Swap all screens from currentIssues to filteredIssues
description: One-line change per screen (project_dashboard.dart, tree_view_screen.dart, kanban_screen.dart, ready_queue_screen.dart, blocked_screen.dart, dependency_graph_screen.dart): read appState.filteredIssues instead of appState.currentIssues as the base list before each screen's existing status/blocked-ness .where() chain. Also add the FilterChipBar widget into each screen's layout, between ToolBar and ContentArea.
close_reason: Swapped the base issue list from appState.currentIssues to appState.filteredIssues in project_dashboard.dart, tree_view_screen.dart, kanban_screen.dart, ready_queue_screen.dart, blocked_screen.dart, and dependency_graph_screen.dart (existing status/blocked-ness .where() chains now run on top of the filtered set). tree_view_screen.dart's _expandAll helper intentionally still reads currentIssues since it's a full-tree-structure utility, not a display list. Wired FilterChipBar into each screen's ContentArea builder as a fixed row above the scrollable/list content (Column([FilterChipBar(), Expanded(child: body)])) since MacosScaffold.children only accepts ResizablePane/ContentArea, not arbitrary siblings — this satisfies GEMINI.md's 'second row between ToolBar and ContentArea' requirement within that constraint. dart analyze clean across all 6 files.

title: Wire AppLogger to macOS os_log for native Console.app integration
description:
AppLogger currently uses dart:developer log() + debugPrint as its substrate. In release builds only WARNING+ reaches stdout, and that output is unstructured in Console.app (no subsystem/category/level filtering). For proper macOS unified logging integration:

1. Add a native Swift method channel handler (macos/Runner/AppDelegate.swift or a dedicated plugin) that calls os_log with subsystem 'wtf.ghc.watcher' and a per-category OSLog instance:
   import os
   let log = OSLog(subsystem: "wtf.ghc.watcher", category: category)
   os_log(.error, log: log, "%{public}@", message)

2. Update AppLogger._emit to invoke the channel for WARNING+ in release mode (and optionally in debug mode too):
   await _logChannel.invokeMethod('log', {
     'level': level,
     'category': name,  // e.g. 'BeadsService', 'TmuxService'
     'message': message,
     'error': error?.toString(),
   });

3. In Console.app users can then filter by:
   - Subsystem: wtf.ghc.watcher
   - Category: BeadsService / AppState / TmuxService / etc.
   - Level: Error / Fault / Info

4. Keep the dart:developer path for DevTools visibility during development — the two are complementary.

5. Consider whether DEBUG/INFO should also route to os_log (at .debug/.info level) so they appear in Console.app when the 'Include Debug Messages' option is enabled.

Note: os_log entries persist in the unified log for up to a few days and survive app crashes, making them invaluable for diagnosing issues in the installed release app that cannot be reproduced with DevTools attached.

title: Author dependencies from the UI (blocks/related)
description: Today drag-and-drop only sets a parent (tree_node.dart) and issue creation hardcodes parent-child. Add: an inspector '+ Add dependency' affordance (pick type + target issue), and/or modifier-drag in the tree to create a 'blocks' edge instead of reparenting. Requires a daemon RPC / bd dep add path for non-parent-child links. Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.
close_reason: Go daemon: add_dependency + remove_dependency RPC handlers. BeadsService: addDependency/removeDependency. AppState: addDependency/removeDependency. Inspector: 'Add dependency' button opening a sheet to pick type + target with live ID validation.

title: New view: Dependency graph (DAG) visualization
description: Replace the dead GraphNode path (issue.dart:68-85, beads_service getGraph() returns empty wrappers) with a real DAG view as a new screen alongside Tree/Kanban/Dashboard, showing 'blocks' edges that cross the parent-child tree -- the structure read-aloud actually demands. Consider leaning on bd graph / bd dep tree output rather than a from-scratch layout engine. Larger effort; sequence after P1/P2 land. Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.
close_reason: New DependencyGraphScreen at /graph: expandable root-blocker chains with cascade of blocked dependents. Summary bar + indirect impediments section.

title: Spike: evaluate libghostty embedded terminal vs AppleScript bridge
description: Research replacing the open -na + AppleScript bridge with an embedded libghostty terminal to eliminate AppleScript fragility (injection, racy delays, wrong-window targeting). Assess libghostty C API maturity (pre-1.0), FFI integration cost with Flutter/macOS, and whether the in-app terminal UX is a real goal. Output a recommendation.

title: AI Summarization of Closed Tasks
description: Implement an async summarization pipeline. 1) Add a Global Setting to configure the Gemini model ID and region (defaulting to a fast lite model). 2) Introduce an automated summarizer that kicks off when a task is closed, utilizing the selected model to summarize how the task was resolved based on git diffs/context, and posts that summary as a comment or metadata field to be displayed in the Activity Ticker.
close_reason: Implemented dynamic AI model management, Vertex AI region support, and automated issue resolution summarization.

title: Design Sidebar Notifications and Dynamic Sorting
description:
Investigate and implement macOS HIG-compliant notification patterns for the Sidebar. Ideas:
1. **Unread Badges:** When a background sync or agent task mutates a project's database (detected via events.jsonl), display a small systemBlue circular badge next to the project name if it is not currently selected. This requires AppState to track a 'lastViewedTimestamp' per project.
2. **Dynamic Sorting:** Add a User Preference to sort the Sidebar project list either 'Alphabetically' or 'By Recent Activity'. The 'Recent Activity' sort would bubble projects with new interactions to the top, similar to the macOS Messages or Mail app. 
3. **Activity Previews:** Consider replacing the generic folder/cloud icon with a subtle '1m ago' timestamp when in the 'Recent Activity' sort mode.
close_reason: Implemented dynamic sidebar sorting, unread activity badges, and activity previews with persistent user settings.

comments.author: watcher-agent
comments.text: Phase 1 complete: Implemented the unread blue dot indicators based on the events.jsonl timestamps (tracked in jn9.1). Still remaining: Dynamic sorting and activity previews.

A lower-risk stepping stone if full label editing (Phase 2) feels too heavyweight for a given use case: show 'Suggested labels: X, Y' in the Inspector with a click-to-copy 'bd label add' command, never mutating directly.

title: Cross-label Flow Matrix view (which labels block which labels)
description: Inspired by beads_viewer's Flow Matrix view — a cross-label dependency matrix showing which labels' issues block which other labels' issues, surfacing team/domain coupling that a single dependency graph doesn't make visible at a glance. Candidate as an alternate 'zoomed out' mode on the existing Dependency Graph screen rather than a new route.

title: Phase 4 (Backlog/Roadmap): Label analytics — health, suggestions, flow
description: NOT COMMITTED. Candidate roadmap items surfaced by the beads_viewer competitive analysis (docs/LABEL_AND_FEATURE_COMPARISON_beads_viewer.md), kept separate from Phases 1-3 because they are pure read-side analytics rather than core label UX — revisit priority after Phases 1-3 ship and real usage/feedback comes in. All three ideas are independently reimplemented concepts inspired by observing bv's UX, not derived from its source (bv's license carries a rider restricting Anthropic/OpenAI-affiliated use of its code — see comparison report's Open Questions section for the full disclosure).
